steps.title: Identify the structures on each side of the blank
steps.content: On the left side is an independent clause: “the guide soon grew in scope and influence.” On the right side is another independent clause: “its modest French eateries were replaced with some of the most esteemed restaurants from around the world.”

steps.title: Apply the rule for connecting independent clauses
steps.content: A comma plus a coordinating conjunction joins two independent clauses. Choice C provides both elements; the other choices violate this rule by including only the comma (A), only the conjunction (B), or neither (D), producing a comma splice or fused sentence. That matches choice C — C is correct.
